commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an optional `timeFormat` Control UI bootstrap field, stores it in UI formatter state, applies it to shared timestamp formatting and WebChat timestamps, and adds focused gateway/UI tests.

PR surface: Source +32, Tests +102. Total +134 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main defines and documents `agents.defaults.timeFormat`, but the Control UI timestamp helpers and WebChat group timestamp renderer use browser-locale formatting without consuming that setting.
commands.review_followup: - No code repair is indicated; the remaining action is normal maintainer review plus any needed proof-check refresh.

target.title: fix(control-ui): respect agents.defaults.timeFormat for timestamps
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes the Control UI / WebChat rendering timestamps with a hardcoded locale clock, ignoring the existing `agents.defaults.timeFormat` setting (showing `7:38 PM` when the operator configured 24-hour).
- Threads the resolved `agents.defaults.timeFormat` through the gateway bootstrap config into a small UI hour-cycle helper, so `formatMs` / `formatTimeMs` / `formatDateTimeMs` and the WebChat group timestamp all honor `12` / `24`.
- Leaves `auto` (and unset) as the browser locale default, so existing deployments render exactly as before — only an explicit `12`/`24` forces an hour cycle.

## Linked context

Closes #58147

The config key already exists (`src/config/types.agent-defaults.ts`, `agents.defaults.timeFormat: "auto" | "12" | "24"`) and is documented as controlling 12h/24h rendering; the Control UI was simply not wired to it. No new config surface is added — one optional bootstrap field.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Control UI / WebChat timestamps respect `agents.defaults.timeFormat`.
- Real environment tested: a real local OpenClaw gateway (`pnpm dev gateway run`, version **2026.6.2**, local mode, DeepSeek V4 Pro agent) serving the real built Control UI, opened in a real Chromium browser against an existing `main` session.
- Exact steps run after this patch:
  1. Set `agents.defaults.timeFormat: "24"` in `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json`, started the gateway, opened the Control UI, and read the rendered chat timestamps from the live DOM + screenshot.
  2. Changed the setting to `"12"`, restarted the gateway, reloaded, and captured the same session again.
- Evidence after fix — the real gateway emits the field on its bootstrap endpoint:

```text
$ curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer <redacted>" http://127.0.0.1:18789/control-ui-config.json
# with agents.defaults.timeFormat="24":
{ "basePath": "", "assistantName": "...", "serverVersion": "2026.6.2", "timeFormat": "24", ... }
# with agents.defaults.timeFormat="12":
{ ... "serverVersion": "2026.6.2", "timeFormat": "12", ... }
```

- Evidence after fix — the same session's `.chat-group-timestamp` elements read from the live Control UI DOM:

```text
timeFormat = "24"  ->  ["Jun 14, 2026, 19:38", "Jun 14, 2026, 19:39"]
timeFormat = "12"  ->  ["Jun 14, 2026, 7:38 PM", "Jun 14, 2026, 7:39 PM"]
```

- Screenshots (real Control UI v2026.6.2, same session, only `agents.defaults.timeFormat` changed): 24-hour shows `Jun 14, 2026, 19:38` / `19:39`; 12-hour shows `Jun 14, 2026, 7:38 PM` / `7:39 PM`. (Attached on the PR.)
- Observed result after fix: the rendered timestamp flips between 24-hour and 12-hour exactly as configured. With the setting absent (`auto`), rendering matches the prior browser-locale default (verified: no hour-cycle override emitted).
- What was not tested: per-agent `timeFormat` overrides are intentionally out of scope — this fix consumes the global `agents.defaults` value the issue names. The bootstrap field is optional, so old/missing payloads keep current behavior.

## Tests and validation

- `git diff --check`; `pnpm format:check` (changed files clean).
- Type lanes green: `tsgo:core`, `tsgo:test:ui`, `tsgo:test:src`.
- Focused tests: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/format.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/control-ui-bootstrap.test.ts` and `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts`.
- Build: `pnpm ui:build` passes locally; CI `build-artifacts` and `check-prod-types` are green.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes` — Control UI timestamps now follow `agents.defaults.timeFormat`; `auto`/unset is unchanged from today.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No` — reuses the existing `agents.defaults.timeFormat` key; the bootstrap payload field is additive and optional, safe for old/missing payloads.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`.

Highest-risk area: the centralized hour-cycle merge inside the shared `format.ts` helpers affects every Control UI timestamp surface (cron, usage, activity, chat).

How is that risk mitigated? `auto`/unset resolves to an empty options object, so behavior is byte-identical to current rendering unless an operator explicitly sets `12`/`24`; caller-supplied options still override the resolved cycle.

Current review state: real behavior proof added (real gateway + real Control UI); awaiting maintainer review and CI.

AI-assisted (Claude Code). Proof above was run manually against a real local gateway and browser.
target.author: zengwen-dt
